name: Cognee
last_major_release: v1.0.0.dev0 — released 2026-04-16 (first v1.0 dev preview); v0.5.8 — released 2026-04-11 (last stable 0.x)
activity: ~16,700 GitHub stars, ~1,700 forks (April 2026); ~141 contributors; commits daily (last commit 1 day ago); 38 open issues; active v0.5.x + v1.0.dev release cadence
storage_backend: multiple configurable — vector stores: LanceDB (default, zero-setup), Qdrant, PGVector, Redis (RedisVL), ChromaDB, Weaviate, Milvus, FalkorDB; graph stores: KuzuDB (default embedded), Neo4j, FalkorDB, Memgraph, Neptune, NetworkX; relational metadata: SQLite/Postgres
representation_primitive: entity + edge (KG) + document chunk + vector embedding combined. The cognify pipeline produces simultaneously: chunked text, entity nodes with LLM-inferred or schema-prescribed type labels, typed relationship edges, summary nodes, and per-chunk vector embeddings stored across graph + vector stores. Custom Graph Models (Pydantic) enable domain-specific entity/relationship schemas.
api_shape: Python library (pip install cognee) + REST/FastAPI server + CLI + first-party MCP server (cognee-mcp in repo; also Enhanced Cognee MCP on LobeHub). Docker deployment available.
hosting: both — self-host (local, Docker, Modal, Railway, Fly.io, Render, Daytona) or Cognee Cloud (managed); free tier details not confirmed
llm_dependency: both (ingest + retrieval) — ingest: LLM required for entity/relationship extraction in cognify (Instructor or BAML structured output, multiple calls per document); retrieval: LLM optionally used for query routing and answer synthesis. Default: OpenAI. Routed through LiteLLM supporting OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, AWS Bedrock, Ollama.
schema_extensibility: prescribed+learned hybrid — default KnowledgeGraph Pydantic model does open LLM-driven extraction; Custom Graph Models define domain-specific entity/relationship schemas (prescribed); ontology-based fuzzy validation (FuzzyMatchingStrategy, difflib cutoff 0.80) enforces consistency; Cascade schema expansion grows schema from anchor points data-driven
schema_richness: Default: unconstrained (LLM-inferred entity and relation names). With Custom Graph Model: developer-defined with any count of entity/relationship types. CodeGraph pipeline provides: File, Function, Class, Module nodes with CALLS, IMPORTS, DEFINES, DEPENDS_ON edges. No published fixed taxonomy approaching Gimle §5's 20 entities / 15 edges, but Custom Graph Models can be designed to reach that. Multi-label: not explicitly documented. Multi-graph: dataset-scoped partitioning with per-dataset graph/vector sub-stores.
temporal_model: [uncertain] — no published bi-temporal model; memify post-processing prunes stale nodes and reweights edges based on usage signals (recency-aware but not bi-temporal); session memory provides fast cache sync to graph; no valid_from/valid_to/recorded_at timestamps documented
forgetting_policy: agent-driven-deletion + LLM-reflection-based pruning — memify pipeline prunes stale nodes, strengthens frequent connections, reweights edges by usage, adds derived facts; explicit forget() operation for deletion
ingest_latency_class: batch-offline — cognify runs 6 sequential tasks (classify, permissions check, chunk, LLM extract graph, summarize, embed+store); multiple LLM calls per document chunk; seconds-to-minutes per corpus. Session memory (fast cache) provides near-real-time transient context.
retrieval_modes: hybrid — graph traversal + vector similarity + optional BM25; auto-routing via recall() selects graph or vector path per query; time-filter support; community search not confirmed
retrieval_agenticity: multi-hop + policy-guided (partial) — recall() auto-routes between graph traversal and vector search; graph traversal enables multi-hop relationship following; Hermes Agent integration for agentic reasoning. No explicit Self-RAG or CRAG loop documented.
retrieval_budget_control: no — no first-class per-query token/hop budget parameters documented
multi_project_namespace: dataset-scoped partitioning — each dataset is an isolated unit with its own graph and vector sub-stores; Tenant containers for organization-level isolation; role-based dataset sharing; cross-dataset queries not confirmed
multi_user_access: RBAC — built-in user management: Tenants (org-level containers), Roles (Analyst, Contributor etc.), per-user permission overrides; dataset-scoped access control; agentic user/tenant isolation with audit traits and OTEL collector
embedder_options: built-in: OpenAI (default, large text embeddings), Ollama (nomic-embed-text 768 dims and others), AWS Bedrock; all routed through LiteLLM. Custom embedding model via env vars (EMBEDDING_MODEL, EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS). Falls back to LLM_API_KEY if EMBEDDING_API_KEY not set.
llm_client_options: LiteLLM proxy routes all LLM calls — OpenAI (default), Anthropic, Google Gemini (prefix gemini/model-name), AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Ollama (local). LLM cannot be disabled (entity extraction in cognify requires LLM). No structured-writes-only mode.
cost_privacy: Multiple LLM calls per document at ingest (extract + summarize + embed). Ollama enables fully local deployment with no data leaving host. Cloud deployment sends data to Cognee Cloud or configured LLM provider. Token cost per document not published. OTEL collector available for cost observability.
code_artifact_linkage: symbol + AST-node via first-party CodeGraph pipeline — run_code_graph_pipeline() parses Python AST to extract: File nodes, Function nodes, Class nodes, Module nodes; edges: CALLS, IMPORTS, DEFINES, DEPENDS_ON. Extension to JavaScript/TypeScript/Java/Go is planned (issue #1160, open). Issue-PR-to-symbol chain: not natively supported.
code_analysis_features: First-party: call graph (CALLS edges), symbol index (Function/Class nodes), import resolution (IMPORTS edges), dependency graph (DEPENDS_ON edges). Planned: multi-language support (JS/TS/Java/Go). Not first-party: diff-awareness, type-aware analysis, cross-language linking, issue-PR-to-symbol chain.
memory_os_layer: plaintext — structured KG + vector store; session memory adds a fast transient cache layer (syncs to graph in background); no parametric (weight-tuning) or activation (KV-cache) layer
self_improvement_mechanism: LLM-reflection + online-alias-learning — memify post-processing refines graph after ingest (prune stale, strengthen frequent, reweight edges, add derived facts); Cascade schema expansion grows schema from data feedback; skill routing improves from historical success patterns; explicit improve() operation
provenance_standard: OpenTelemetry — OTEL collector integration with agentic user/tenant isolation and audit traits; custom-log also present; no W3C-PROV
benchmark_results: No published LongMemEval or LoCoMo scores as of April 2026. Sales Benchmark Demo introduced in v0.5.8rc1 for internal evaluation only. Third-party (vectorize.io, April 2026): Cognee has no official LongMemEval score — direct comparison with Mem0 (93.4) not possible. Code understanding quality not benchmarked against CrossCodeEval or SWE-bench.
strengths: 1. First-party CodeGraph pipeline (AST-based) with File/Function/Class/Module nodes and CALLS/IMPORTS/DEFINES/DEPENDS_ON edges — only reviewed system with native code-understanding layer; most directly relevant to Gimle's Symbol/File/Module graph needs. 2. Prescribed+learned hybrid schema (Custom Graph Models with Pydantic) enables Gimle-style domain schema while retaining LLM discovery for uncategorized facts. 3. RBAC multi-tenancy with OTEL tracing is production-grade for multi-agent team environments.
weaknesses: 1. No bi-temporal model — Gimle §5 requires valid_from/valid_to + recorded_at; Cognee's memify pruning is usage-based not temporally-modeled; stale-node pruning could delete facts Gimle needs to retain historically. 2. High LLM cost at ingest — multiple LLM calls per document chunk makes real-time per-commit ingest expensive; batch-offline model conflicts with Gimle's incremental episode ingest requirement. 3. No published retrieval benchmark — quality vs Mem0 (93.4) and Zep/Graphiti unverified; CodeGraph accuracy on production codebases not benchmarked.
fit_with_gimle_section_5: Verdict: steal-specific-ideas — CodeGraph pipeline design is the most directly adoptable component for Gimle's code extractor. Key ideas to steal: (1) CodeGraph node/edge taxonomy: run_code_graph_pipeline() as template for Gimle's code-understanding extractor. Taxonomy maps cleanly to Gimle §5 targets: (:File {path, language})-[:DEFINES]->(:Function {name, signature}); (:Function)-[:CALLS]->(:Function); (:File)-[:IMPORTS]->(:Module). Extend for Gimle: (:Symbol)-[:REFERENCED_IN]->(:Decision), (:Iteration)-[:MODIFIES]->(:File), (:PR)-[:CHANGES]->(:Symbol). (2) Custom Graph Models (Pydantic) as schema definition pattern: directly transferable to Graphiti's entity type system. Gimle can define: class GimleKG(KnowledgeGraph): entities: list[Union[Project, Module, File, Symbol, Decision, Iteration]]; edges: list[Union[DefinesEdge, CallsEdge, ImportsEdge, DecidesInEdge]]. (3) RBAC + OTEL pattern: Cognee's Tenant/Role/Dataset isolation model informs Gimle's group_id + agent namespace + audit trail design. Not adopt-whole: no bi-temporal model (critical blocker for Gimle §5), batch-offline ingest conflicts with incremental episode model, Graphiti already provides better temporal + entity-edge primitives for Gimle's core layer.
